mappings.rationaleAI: The Inca Empire covers the largest pre-Columbian civilization in the Americas including Pizarro's conquest, directly matching A on geography and ethnography of the Americas and B.1 on Spanish discovery, exploration, and conquest.
The Aztec Empire covers Tenochtitlan, the Triple Alliance, chinampas agriculture, and the Spanish conquest under Cortes, directly matching A on ethnography and B.1 on Spanish conquest of Mexico.

Mercantilism covers colonial exploitation, Navigation Acts, and chartered trading companies, directly matching B.2.c on colonial mercantilist economy and C.3.b.i on British economic policies including the Navigation Acts.

The Jesuits' missionary work in South America including the Reductions directly matches B.2.b on the missionary role of the Roman Catholic Church in Spanish colonies and B.3.b on the role of the Church in colonial Brazil.
The Columbian Exchange and introduction of New World foods (potatoes, tomatoes, chocolate) and the colonial plantation economy directly relate to B.2.c on colonial economy and agricultural expansion.
The colonial coffee plantation economy in the Caribbean and Americas directly relates to B.2.c on colonial economic development and agricultural trade.

Washington and the American Revolution cover the political opposition to Britain, the War of Independence, the Constitutional Convention, and republican government, directly matching C.3 on the development of the English colonies and their conflict with Britain (though the Revolution extends beyond this section's scope to 1763).
The Franco-American Alliance of 1778 covers France's entry into the American Revolutionary War, matching the context of C.3.d on conflicts with the French and expulsion of French power.
